evals.prompt: I have 20 ONT WGS BAMs that I need to run modkit pileup on, restricted to a BED file of CpG islands. Can you create a Snakemake workflow for this? The BAMs are listed in a sample sheet TSV at /data1/greenbab/projects/my_project/samples.tsv with columns 'sample' and 'bam_path'. The BED file uses bare chromosome names (1, 2, ...) but the BAMs use chr-prefixed names. I want to use the onttools_v3.9.sif container.
evals.expected_output: A complete Snakefile with: chr prefix conversion rule, modkit pileup rule with --include-bed, proper singularity: IMG directive, mem_mb: 0 in profile, benchmark: directives, log: directives under LOGDIR, all outputs under RESULTSDIR, config_template.yaml, and run_snakemake.sh with unset SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE.

assertions.name: has_mem_mb_zero
assertions.description: Profile must zero out built-in mem_mb to avoid SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE conflict

assertions.name: has_slurm_account
assertions.description: Profile must include slurm_account to avoid silent job rejection

assertions.name: has_singularity_directive
assertions.description: Rules needing container packages must have singularity: directive

assertions.name: has_benchmark_directive
assertions.description: Compute-heavy rules must have benchmark: directive

assertions.name: single_output_dir
assertions.description: Must use single output_dir config key, not separate dirs

assertions.name: chr_prefix_handling
assertions.description: Must handle chr prefix conversion between BED and BAM

evals.prompt:
My Snakemake workflow keeps failing with 'Fatal: SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE and SLURM_MEM_PER_CPU are mutually exclusive'. I'm using snakemake 9 with the SLURM executor plugin. Here's my profile:

executor: slurm
default-resources:
  slurm_partition: componc_cpu
  mem_mb_per_cpu: 16000
  cpus_per_task: 4

What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis: missing mem_mb: 0 in default-resources. Snakemake 9 injects a built-in mem_mb: 1000 default which sets SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE, conflicting with mem_mb_per_cpu. Fix: add mem_mb: 0 to default-resources. Also check if coordinator was submitted with --mem (should use --mem-per-cpu instead) and add unset SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE to run script.

assertions.name: explains_root_cause
assertions.value: Snakemake injects a built-in mem_mb default that conflicts with mem_mb_per_cpu
assertions.description: Must explain WHY the conflict occurs

evals.prompt: I have a working Snakemake pipeline with 3 rules (align, call_variants, annotate). I want to add a QC gate after alignment that checks mapping rate is above 80% before variant calling proceeds. The flagstat output is at results/alignment/{sample}/{sample}.flagstat. How should I structure this?
evals.expected_output: A QC gate rule that reads the flagstat file, extracts mapping rate, raises ValueError if <80%, writes a .pass sentinel file to qc/ directory. The call_variants rule should depend on both the BAM and the .pass file. QC rule should have retries: 0 (data issue, not transient).

assertions.name: has_pass_sentinel
assertions.description: QC gate must produce a .pass sentinel file

assertions.name: has_retries_zero
assertions.description: QC gates should have retries: 0 (data issues, not transient)

assertions.name: downstream_depends_on_qc
assertions.value: downstream rule depends on the QC pass file
assertions.description: Variant calling must depend on QC sentinel

assertions.name: qc_dir_convention
assertions.description: QC outputs should go to qc/ directory
